concerns.text: Task T012 implements 'ICA-based artifact rejection with automatic component classification' but omits the 'manual review capability via log file or visual inspection interface' mandated by FR-003. This is a semantic weakening of the requirement, as automatic classification alone does not satisfy the explicit need for human-in-the-loop verification.

Task T032 instructs validation 'against SC-001 through SC-006 thresholds'. SC-002 explicitly defers the accuracy benchmark value to the research phase (marked '[deferred]') and states it is 'not a claim of scientific superiority'. A task cannot validate against a non-existent threshold; this creates an impossible execution condition and silently relaxes the requirement to 'report metrics' rather than 'pass/fail against benchmark'.

Task T014 defines logic: 'halt if <50 epochs/condition, warn if <100'. This contradicts the spec's Edge Cases and SC-005 which require a specific 'underpowered' label for the intermediate range (50-99) and a halt only if <50. The task description lacks the 'underpowered' labeling logic, effectively dropping a specific acceptance criterion for handling underpowered scenarios.
